headline: Pseudo-labeling Bayesian prior run finished at 0.665 val AUC, narrowly below leader at 0.673.
focus: Determine whether the pseudo-labeling approach has enough signal to pursue further or should be retired in favor of the class-coverage-fix leader.
root_cause: Pseudo-labeling unfitted classes from the Bayesian prior produced only a marginal +0.00013 improvement over the original baseline (0.6649 vs 0.6651), and is -0.0076 behind the current leader (class-coverage-fix at 0.6727). The probe overfitting pattern persists: train soundscape AUC 0.992 vs val 0.665. The Bayesian prior is paradoxically stronger on validation (0.662) than train (0.487), confirming the probe head itself remains the bottleneck. Pseudo-labeling did not meaningfully close the coverage or overfitting gap.
finding_titles: Pseudo-label unfitted classes from Bayesian prior completed
